text.title: claude-tool-use Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for claude-tool-use. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Write tool descriptions as if explaining to a colleague who has never seen your codebase — specificity prevents wrong tool selection; vague descriptions lead to wrong tool calls with no error.
rule.rationale: Tool descriptions are the primary signal for tool selection. The model has no other context for choosing between tools.

rule.statement: Check stop_reason == "tool_use" explicitly on every response; missing this check causes the agent loop to exit early on tool requests.
rule.rationale: tool_use is a stop, not an error. The API returns stop_reason="tool_use" whenever Claude wants to call a tool; the caller must handle it.

rule.statement: Set a max-iterations guard (10–20 turns); tool loops without a limit run indefinitely when a tool returns a consistently unhelpful result.
rule.rationale: Infinite loops burn quota and can be triggered by a single broken tool implementation that always returns the same response.

rule.statement: Append response.content (the full list including tool_use blocks) as the assistant message, not just the text. Omitting tool_use blocks breaks conversation history and causes API errors on the next turn.
rule.rationale: The API requires complete message history including tool_use and tool_result blocks to reconstruct the conversation state.

rule.statement: Execute all tool_use blocks from a single response concurrently — do not serialize what Claude dispatched in parallel; return all results in one user message.
rule.rationale: Claude requests parallel tools to reduce round-trips; serializing them defeats the optimization and increases latency.

rule.statement: Return tool errors as content (not is_error=True) unless the error should terminate the loop — Claude can often recover from tool errors when they're returned as data.
rule.rationale: is_error=True signals an unrecoverable error; most tool failures (timeouts, not-found) are recoverable if Claude sees the error message as data.

rule.statement: To get guaranteed typed output, define a single extraction tool and force it with tool_choice={"type": "tool", "name": "..."}; access the result from tool_use.input.
rule.rationale: Prompt engineering ("return only JSON") fails occasionally even with temperature=0. The forced tool call routes through the tool use pathway which is more reliable for structured data.

rule.statement: MCP is primarily designed for Claude Desktop (stdio transport); programmatic API usage should use standard tool use, not MCP.
rule.rationale: MCP servers communicate via stdio by default and require subprocess lifecycle management; standard tool use is stateless and easier to operationalize in servers.
